title: Level 100.6
wikitext: {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 100.6: "Slow Erosion"}}__NOTOC__ {{Background|file=747.jpg}} {{Class 4e}} '''Level 100.6''' is a sub-level of [[Level 100]] and is a significant environmental and mental hazard to those who may enter it. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=Liminal beach by arfiansyah22 dfldzpt-pre.jpg|caption=A rare image of the sky before a Spire Event.}} '''Level 100.6''' seems to be a sandy beach under a cloudy sky, stretching 20–30 meters wide. On the left side is a sheer cliff that ends with sharp rocks. Farther than this cliff is a frigid ocean with below-zero temperatures. It is unknown how the water is below-zero, but is not frozen over. On the right side, there is a patch of thick brush. Entering this thick brush is not advised, as it is unknown what happens to those who do. The water is either calmly lapping at the cliffs or crashing into them—it depends on the state of the tides. This beach is decidedly empty, and this may contribute to feelings of loneliness. Wave sickness will add to this as wanderers progress deeper into this level, and some may be overcome by this feeling. This mental hazard alone would make it a deadly level, but the main cause for concern is the anomalies on this level. The temperature on this level’s beach is mild, unlike the freezing water. A wanderer entering this level will be separated from any groups they were previously with. They will feel a sense of dread encompassing them—this being the beginning of the wave sickness. This wave sickness is the first hazard that wanderers will encounter in this level. The waves create a repetitive crashing sound that gradually deteriorates wanderers’ mental stability—causing dizziness, slurred speech, and ultimately, insanity. For some unknown reason, anybody with wave sickness has been shown to have severe encephalitis. Encephalitis is an often-fatal condition in which the brain swells and creates pressure against itself. There are tides on this level, with low tide exposing sharp rocks near the bottom of the cliffs. The Spires event is far more common during low tide, according to most survivor accounts. Though the calm waters may delay wave sickness, this fragile reprieve comes at a cost—Spire events become alarmingly frequent. At high tide, the waves are crashing, and the currents in the water are much stronger. There have been extremely fast and strong rip currents recorded, and getting in the water is exceedingly dangerous and unadvised when the tide is near the top of the cliffs. Strangely, high tide has been recorded to be much more common than low tide. While this may serve as a detriment to wanderers, it does make the Spire event much rarer. It is theorized that this entire level will be either inhospitable for human life or destroyed in the near future. This is because at high tide, the waves slowly erode the sand into the water. This causes the stability of the sand near the shore to be low, and it is unadvised to approach the edge. {{Blurimage|file=747.jpg|caption=A beach at high tide an hour before a thunderstorm.}} ==Anomalies== '''Level 100.6''' is known to have many dangerous anomalies. It is speculated that there may be more than these listed anomalies, but due to the limited information from survivors, these are the only anomalies that can be documented with confidence. {{Blurimage|file=953158-lightning-storm-rain-clouds-sky-nature-thunderstorm.jpg|caption=The early stages of a thunderstorm on the horizon.}} '''Thunderstorms:''' The horizon overlooking the frigid waters will normally be cloudy, with slight and rare rumbles of thunder. Over time, these will become more and more frequent, and then the first anomaly may occur. Without warning, the sky will open up, and a torrent of rain will begin to fall. Temperature will rapidly drop, and winds of up to 60 MPH will begin. This will cause wanderers instant distress and may even contribute to casualties. Being in the water during this time is a death sentence, as lightning will rapidly strike the water. The world will become extremely dark, with the lightning as the only thing to illuminate the beach. These thunderstorms are much more powerful than anything ever recorded in [[The Frontrooms|reality]]. '''Spires:''' This is the other anomaly on this level, and one of much more danger. This event will be preceded by all clouds going away and a sense of hope filling the wanderer. This usually occurs after a thunderstorm. To survive, it is advised to instantly leave the level. After this, black jagged spires of inky black material will rise from the horizon. Wanderers will feel the urge to stare at these spires. These spires will continue to grow after erupting from the ground, and once they reach the clouds, something very mysterious will happen. A cacophony—described by some as agonized screaming and others as angelic singing—will fill the air. It is unknown what happens to wanderers who remain on this level when this sound fills the air, but only three very lucky individuals have survived hearing this sound. No images of this event have ever been recorded, and any images taken near this event are distorted, as seen with the earlier picture. '''Theories:''' There are many theories on the nature of these spires. Some believe that they are entities unique to this level, and others believe they are part of the level. The main debate concerns what they do to people, and therefore, how to counteract it. Some people think that the sound drives people to jump onto the rocks. Evidence supporting this conclusion is that the event is more likely to transpire at low tide, when rocks are exposed and more dangerous. Others think it simply absorbs them into the level. The fate of those who succumb to the spires is unknown. ==Entities == The presence of entities is highly unlikely, unless we consider the '''Spires''' themselves as entities. This belief is most likely incorrect, as the '''Spires''' do not act like entities but more as part of the level. Some survivors report seeing massive shapes in the water at high tide, but this is believed to be a hallucination. Even if it is, this serves to discourage wanderers from entering the water further. ==Entrances and Exits== === Entrances === There are few entrances to this level, and only one is consistent. * Noclipping into a puddle on [[Level 1]] may bring wanderers here, though the chances are very low. * Seeing a spire just out of the ocean on [[Level 100]] may noclip a wanderer here. * Another rare entrance is to find an out of place empty bathtub in [[Level 19]] and jump into it. * Rarely a wanderer who is hallucinating due to the [[Level 100]] Illness may noclip here. ===Exits=== Exits to this level are generally very dangerous and should only be used as a last resort. * Swimming about a mile out into the water at high tide will bring one to [[Level 353]]. This exit is unadvised because of the deadly rip currents that can occur at high tides. * Jumping onto the rocks during the '''Spires''' event may transport one to [[Level 100]], but it also may end in the person's death. * Finding an inky black puddle on the beach and touching it will lead to [[Level 6]]. * If a dark fog rolls in from the sea, any wanderer caught within it will be transported to [[Level 287]]. The fog appears without warning and envelops the entire beach within minutes. * At low tide, a single colorful pool float may appear drifting offshore. Swimming up to it and touching it will lead the wanderer to [[Level 30]]. The purpose and origin of the float remain unknown, and its appearance is rare and unpredictable. {{Redirect|Prev=100|Next=101}} {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body='''Author''': [[User:CodenameKraken|CodenameKraken]]}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Aquatic]] [[Category:Sub-Levels]] [[Category:Dangerous]]
